title: What I think about the Economy and how to fix it 1/2

transcript.text: Hello, I am Kyle Pounds, and this video is how to fix the economy.
Well, yes, we're in hard financial times, so I think we should use this as a wake-up call,
Opportunity to change our trajectory.
I think we're in bad financial times because of the war in Iraq.
But also because of over-building, over-borrowing from everybody.
And then, kind of, and I think that was like building and borrowing and all this stuff
For building up, like, strip malls, stores, and this kind of thing,
Kind of geared towards our culture of consumerism.
You know, it's always been kind of the idea of, if you want to fix the economy, we'll go shopping.
Well, you know, that's oversimplified, and I think destructive philosophy to have
To buy something just so that you will have more money to buy more stuff, basically,
Is what that idea is.
And it's blind.
It's not good for the environment to just be constantly building stuff because, you know,
We only have so much room in our houses.
If we keep buying, we're going to have to throw stuff away, and then that fills up the landfills.
And it's, you know, so it's an overuse of resources to build stuff and then filling up landfills
When you can just keep your old stuff.
And I think, and I'm not an economist, I don't go to economy school or anything,
But it seems common sense to me that a strong economy is strong because all of the constituents
Of the people living in that country, the community, are busy.
They're busting their rumps, which creates, and then they can feed off each other and do,
You know, do credit like that.
It doesn't have to be physical stuff.
You don't have to be manufacturing stuff in order to make money off each other.
We just should get, you know, our raw necessities as cheaply as we can
And then try and create as many jobs in a non-consumeristic sector
And encourage that, anyways, and start to disencourage consumerism
Just for the sake of increasing the economy.
There's other reasons for consumerism also.
There's just, there's stupid, like, status.
I think the way people respect each other should be changed a little bit.
I think nowadays people, it kind of seems like it's, you know, socially acceptable
And socially encouraged to start a family.
But we're overpopulated, so if you don't start a family, that should be commended.
And being, like, materialistic, I don't think should be something that should be, like, strived after
And respected, you know, people who have, who just collect stuff, you know, have,
Is that the next thing?
I mean, that's great if somebody wants to do that.
That's awesome.
But I think it should be also encouraged to live simply.
I think the simpler we live, the less materials we use,
The more we can go back into the, live like strong animals back in nature.
And so I think it should be easy for people and accessible for people to live out of their cars.
If people do that, they'll have extra money to spend on other things that are productive to them
And not just spending on maintaining a high overhead house.
The high overhead isn't just money for them, but it's high overhead.
It taxes the community in general.
They'll use more water to water their lawn.
They'll use more everything to fix this, you know.
They'll just use more.
And so if you live out of your car, and I know this sounds kind of crazy,
But I'm just saying that it should be, like, seen as something beautiful
Instead of something that's, like, tragic or unfortunate.
I think the best way to get people to do that would be, like, having movies,
TV shows about people like that, people who live very simply, you know.
And right now, every time you see a movie, it's just about all these, like, rich people, you know.
Like, meet the Fockers, you know.
They go and meet the...
Oh, I guess they didn't have a big, big house, did they?
But I don't know.
It's just movies.
It's all about, like, if it's about somebody, it's about some high-powered person,
Head of a company, or some rich person.
And let's have movies with, like, about just, like, really simply living people.
Like, some person lives out of their car, and they...
But they're involved in some project, you know.
And so they're always out and about, and then this kind of thing, you know.
And because if we just...
The point of me saying this is just so we can get...
Phase out of the consumeristic,
And get used to the idea of creating other kinds of jobs.
I think a good central government is good.
I think it's about time we have a good democratic party in power.
I think right now it's good to have a good central government,
Because we need some consolidation as a country to build,
To quickly build up a very strong, reusable energy grid.
And so the government's the best thing to do that.
And I think the National Highway Program was brilliant.
I do not see...
Well, I think it's...
If, you know...
I think we should have our freezing for the cars,
But I just got sidetracked again.
So, let's see.
Am I done?
Yeah.
So, education.
I think we should put a lot of public money into education.
Because I think people will start to have more time and money and desire
To continue their education after high school.
And it can be an industry.
So, we should definitely encourage that.
Let's see.
As far as worried about other countries, you know,
Like outsourcing, we're losing our country.
And, you know, selling our country to the Chinese,
We're losing our country, and all this stuff.
Like, unfair trade practices.
Like, these are details.
And, you know, if you're going to, like, mention stuff like that,
I think it's good to bring up all the facts.
Because at the end of the day, I don't think...
I think it's kind of selfish to kind of be, like,
Comparing yourself to the Chinese
And be afraid of Chinese taking you over
Or taking advantage of our country.
Because Chinese people live a lot less quality life than we do.
They need to catch up to us.
And India, you know.
I think us outsourcing to India is a really good thing.
I mean, it's less jobs for us,
But they'll work a lot cheaper.
So it's cheaper for us to get tech support,
Which means that we'll get more educated
On computers and technology faster,
Which is good for us.
And it's also good for the Indians
Because it helps them, their economy,
And it also helps them organize
And become tech-savvy themselves.
And so I think it's good.
I think get as globalistic an economy as we possibly can is good.
It's good to think globally,
Not just what's good for your country.
But as far as, like,
How we should deal with big businesses
As opposed to small businesses,
Tax breaks and stuff,
Well, you know, like farming, for example.
I think, well, right now I don't know what it is,
But I know beef people are getting subsidized a lot,
And I don't think they should
Because it's too expensive to grow beef, you know.
